channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Why a Second Dock Fails: A Debugging Guide for USB-C, Thunderbolt, and DisplayLink
item.description: A laptop boots and stops at this message: Multiple docking stations are not simultaneously supported. Sometimes two docks are connected. Sometimes there is one visible dock plus a USB-C monitor...

item.title: The Optimistic UI Race Condition That Only Showed Up on the Fifth Click
item.description: This is a submission for DEV's Summer Bug Smash: Smash Stories powered by Sentry. I originally shared this debugging story on July 8. When Bug Smash was announced, it immediately came to mind...

item.title: The keystroke-counting RTO metric is a confession, not a policy
item.description: A company has recently made a public announcement to admit that, intentionally, they are unable to quantify the work done by engineering. And guess what? The GMO Internet Group discontinued remote...

item.title: DevOps engineers and anyone using Kubernetes - how are you connecting your agents to your production clusters?
item.description: We benchmarked an AI agent on 52 broken clusters: kubectl vs a Kubernetes MCP server Daria Dovzhikova Daria Dovzhikova Daria Dovzhikova Follow Jul 21 We benchmarked an AI agent on 52 broken...

item.title: I REBUILT MY SITE WITH ASTRO & SANITY
item.description: A couple of years ago, when I was deciding which platform to use for web development projects, I was very keen on the idea of using Webflow. It felt like the perfect bridge between the...

item.title: Stop Using Generic AI Review. Build Your Own Board of Experts.
item.description: You finish a piece of work. You ask AI to review it. It says "looks good." You publish. It wasn't good. You had no way to know. That's the problem with adversarial review. You tell the AI to "act...

item.title: I Shipped 5 Products Using These 11 AI Prompts (Open-Sourced, With Outputs)
item.description: I've launched five digital products in the last two months. Every launch artifact — the launch tweet, the Show HN post, the changelog entry, the cold outreach DM, the README intro, the PR...

item.title: Docker for the Developer Who Actually Has to Ship: Secrets, Multi-Stage Builds, Live Reload, and Why Your Container Just Died
item.description: Nephew has Docker basics down now — images, containers, volumes, networks, Compose. But the real world keeps handing him things the fundamentals didn't cover: a container that dies the instant it...

item.title: Snappt Alternative: A Self-Serve PDF Fraud Detection API for Rental & Beyond
item.description: Originally published at htpbe.tech. The version on htpbe.tech stays in sync with the latest detection algorithm — refer to it for the canonical text. If you are searching for a Snappt alternative,...

item.title: Who’s afraid of the big, bad GPU?
item.description: How does AI make you feel? Are you excited to “vibe-code” your smart home? Or anxious about all the added pollution and billions of gallons of water used by data centers? Dig a little deeper and...

item.title: Gritt exits stealth with $34 million for robots to build solar plants—then, everything else
item.description: Gritt is coming out of stealth with $34 million and plan to automate the hardest tasks on construction sites.

item.title: Six UI Testing Problems That Expose Weak Automation
item.description: Most browser automation demos use a familiar workflow: open a page, fill in a form, click Submit, and verify a success message. Real products are rarely that cooperative. The difficult parts are...

item.title: The Army Is Burning Through Its AI Tokens
item.description: Members of the Army received an email informing them that they were rapidly depleting their AI tokens, and needed to limit use.

item.title: Etsy Is In Its Flop Era, and Sellers Are Fleeing
item.description: Once a quirky bastion of amateur vulva jewelry and pet portraits, Etsy is now deluged with mass-produced goods and AI knockoffs. Some customers don’t seem to mind.

item.title: WordPress wp2shell Exploitation Grows as Public Exploit Fuels Mass Scanning
item.description: Attackers have begun to exploit two critical vulnerabilities in WordPress that, when combined together, enable unauthenticated remote code execution (RCE) and complete compromise of vulnerable...

item.title: New ENCFORGE Ransomware Targets AI Model Files in Langflow RCE Attack
item.description: Researchers at Sysdig have linked a second attack on the same Langflow server to JADEPUFFER, the AI-agent-driven operator it first documented earlier this month. The same operator has now been...

item.title: Critical ServiceNow AI Platform Flaw Exploited for Unauthenticated Code Execution
item.description: Threat actors are now exploiting a recently disclosed critical security flaw impacting ServiceNow AI Platform, according to Defused Cyber. In a post shared on X, the threat intelligence firm said...

item.title: Anthropic’s landmark $1.5B copyright settlement is approved
item.description: The final approval settles one case, but it doesn't resolve the broader issue of using copyrighted works to train AI models.

item.title: Grabette: an open system to record robot-manipulation data
Trump’s latest AI czar has already resigned
item.description: The director role for the Center for AI Standards and Innovation (CAISI) has become a revolving door since David Sacks left his position as czar.

item.title: Here are the 30,000 songs Sony is suing Udio’s AI music generator over
item.description: Sony Music Entertainment has filed another lawsuit against Udio, accusing the AI music generator of infringing the copyright of more than 30,000 of its songs, ranging from Elvis Presley's Hound...

item.title: The FCC is planning to retroactively ban disguised DJI gadgets
item.description: Last October, we told you how the FCC had given itself the power to retroactively ban gadgets that have already received its approval to be imported and sold in the United States. Now, the FCC's...

item.title: The Odyssey turned me into an IMAX believer
item.description: After seeing Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey for the first time early last week, I came away impressed, but somewhat conflicted about two of the film's more fantastical set pieces. While those...

item.title: Google is working on a new AI chip designed to make Gemini more efficient
item.description: Alphabet, Google's parent company, is reportedly working on a new chip designed to make its Gemini models run much more efficiently.

item.title: SpaceX in your index fund, explained
item.description: Index funds are touted as one of the safest ways to invest. Rather than picking and choosing individual stocks, index funds let you bet on the market as a whole. So what happens when a company...

item.title: AI’s most important protocol is getting a little bit easier to use
item.description: Under the new system, the protocol will take a looser, "stateless" approach to session IDs on the server side, similar to how most ordinary websites already work.

item.title: Get Borderlands 3, Risk of Rain 2 and 13 other great PC games for $15
item.description: The aptly-named “2K Megahits 2026 Bundle” from Humble includes 15 Steam games for $15. It’s a smattering of the publisher 2K’s biggest games of the 2010s, including BioShock Infinite, Risk of Rain...

item.title: The PlayStation replica ornament is an homage to a great, yet fragile console
item.description: You probably know the signature PlayStation boot sound. Did you know that it's technically a multi-part chime? There's the synthy section where "Sony Computer Entertainment" shows onscreen with a...

item.title: X relaunches a rebuilt Android app after year-long effort
item.description: X says the rebuilt version of its Android app is now available globally.

item.title: OpenAI is scared of open-weight models. Should the US be?
item.description: Talk of banning Chinese-made open-weight LLMs reveals the challenge of turning AI into a business.
